lectures.title: intro hormones

sources.path: Per Lecture/Learning Objectives/end01_LO_Kothmann_Intro to Hormones Objectives.docx
Per Lecture/end01_intro-hormones/01_Endo_2026_Kothmann_Module Intro & Intro to Hormones.pdf

los: Distinguish between endocrine signaling and other forms of intercellular communication
Classify hormones based on their structure, solubility, & storability
Describe catecholamine biosynthesis
Describe the initial step of steroid biosynthesis
Explain the effects of carrier proteins on hormone activity and half-life
Classify hormone receptors based on their subcellular location and mechanism of action
Explain the factors that influence cellular sensitivity to hormones and cellular response to hormones

lectures.title: Hypothalamic pituitary hormones I

sources.path: Per Lecture/Learning Objectives/end02_LO_Kothmann_Hypothalamic-Pituitary Relationships (part 1) Objectives.docx
Per Lecture/end02_Hypothalamic-pituitary-hormones-I/02_Endo_2026_Kothmann_Hypothalamic-Pituitary Relationships (part 1).pdf

lo_missing.lo: Describe the anatomical organization and histology of the pituitary gland, and the cells and structures therein

los: Describe the anatomical connections between the hypothalamus and pituitary gland, and explain the functional interactions between them
Identify the nuclei of the hypothalamus and the hormones they secrete
Describe the anatomical organization and histology of the pituitary gland, and the cells and structures therein
Identify the pituitary hormones, and explain how specific hypothalamic hormones regulate the release of each one
Describe the feedback loops that regulate hormone secretion, and explain the origin and targets for each loop
Predict the changes in hypothalamic, pituitary, and target organ hormone secretion if one particular hormone is increased or decreased (all iterations)

lectures.title: Hypothalamic pituitary hormones II

sources.path: Per Lecture/Learning Objectives/end03_LO_Hypothalamic-Pituitary Relationships (part 2) Objectives.docx
Per Lecture/end03_Hypothalamic-pituitary-hormones-II /03_Endo_2026_Kothmann_Hypothalamic-Pituitary Relationships (part 2).pdf

los: Identify the signaling pathways for growth hormone (GH), from hypothalamus to pituitary to liver to target tissues
Explain the physiological effects of GH and IGF-1, in particular the effects on tissue growth, serum [glucose], and on the liver, adipose tissue, and muscle tissue
Explain how growth hormone secretion is regulated, including dynamic changes on circadian and lifetime timescales
Identify the physiologic stimulators and inhibitors of growth hormone signaling
Describe the effects of altered or disordered growth hormone signaling and the associated conditions

lectures.title: Adrenal Hormone Synthesis

sources.path: Per Lecture/Learning Objectives/end04_LO_Kothmann_Adrenal Hormone Synthesis & Action video Objectives.docx
Per Lecture/end04_Adrenal-Hormone-Synthesis/04_Endo_2026_Kothmann_Adrenal Hormone Synthesis & Action video Objectives.docx
Per Lecture/end04_Adrenal-Hormone-Synthesis/04_Endo_2026_Kothmann_Adrenal Hormone Synthesis & Action_video slides.pdf

los: Recognize the structure of steroid hormones and understand the nomenclature used to describe them
Describe the synthesis steps shared by all steroid hormones
Differentiate between layers of the adrenal cortex, the enzymes that they express, and the hormones that they secrete
Describe the biosynthesis pathways for aldosterone, cortisol, and DHEA
Know the mechanisms that facilitate circulation and inactivation of aldosterone, cortisol, and DHEA, and explain their effects on half-life
Understand the primary mechanisms of action of aldosterone and cortisol on their target cells

lectures.title: sex steroid synthesis

sources.path: Per Lecture/Learning Objectives/end05_LO_Kothmann_Sex Steroid Synthesis & Action video Objectives.docx
Per Lecture/end05_sex-steroid-synthesis/05_Endo_2026_Kothmann_Sex Steroid Synthesis & Action video Objectives.docx
Per Lecture/end05_sex-steroid-synthesis/05_Endo_2026_Kothmann_Sex Steroid Synthesis & Action_video slides.pdf

los: Differentiate between C21 steroids and androgen and estrogen hormones
List the androgen and estrogen hormones and identify their relative affinity for their receptors
Identify the sources of androgen and estrogen hormones
Describe the biosynthesis pathways for androgens and estrogens
Know the mechanisms that facilitate circulation and inactivation of androgens and estrogens, and explain their effects on half-life
Understand the primary mechanisms of action of sex steroid hormones on their target cells

sources.path: Per Lecture/Learning Objectives/end07-LO-Biochem-Sherman_Posterior Pituitary Hormones Objectives.pdf
Per Lecture/Learning Objectives/end08-LO-Path-Santi_Pituitary Pathology Objectives.docx

lectures.title: Thyroid Hormone

sources.path: Per Lecture/Learning Objectives/end10-LO-Biochem-Danielsen_Thyroid Hormone Synthesis and Action notes and Objectives.docx
Per Lecture/end10_Thyroid-Hormone/end10-Danielsen_Thyroid Hormone Synthesis and Action notes.docx

los: Recognize the structure of thyroid hormones, and their precursors.
State the human nutritional requirement for iodide.
State which tissues can take up iodine.
State the cell types and their functions in the thyroid.
Describe the mechanism by which iodide is concentrated in the thyroid.
Give examples of inhibitors of the iodide pump and their mechanism of action.
Describe the enzymes involved in iodide oxidation and organification.
Describe the structure and role of thyroglobulin in thyroid hormone synthesis.
Contrast thyroid hormone storage with storage of other hormones.
Detail mechanisms of thyroglobulin degradation, iodide recycling and hormone release.
Discuss thyroid hormone transport and activation.
State the forms of thyroid hormone receptor and their locations within cells.
Discuss how thyroid hormone receptors activate or inhibit transcription.
Give examples of genes regulated by thyroid hormone.
Give examples of diseases involving hyper- and hypo- thyroid states.

At the end of the course, students should be able to integrate the molecular mechanisms of thyroid hormone synthesis and transcriptional control with the physiological role of thyroid hormone in the human.
orphan_los: Recognize the structure of thyroid hormones, and their precursors.

Give examples of genes regulated by thyroid hormone.
Give examples of diseases involving hyper- and hypo- thyroid states.

At the end of the course, students should be able to integrate the molecular mechanisms of thyroid hormone synthesis and transcriptional control with the physiological role of thyroid hormone in the human.
